🏆 Gamified
How Each Module Works
🃏
Flashcards — HSK 1–6
Flip through 7,500+ vocabulary cards grouped by HSK level (1–6) or by theme. Each card shows the character, pinyin, and meaning. Tap to flip, then rate yourself. Audio mode reads the word aloud first so you identify it by ear. Hard mode hides the pinyin. Cards you miss are automatically queued for spaced-repetition review.
📖
Short Stories — Graded Reading
15 original stories ranging from Beginner (HSK 1–2) to Advanced (HSK 5–6). Each story has line-by-line pinyin and English, a full audio playback button, and a comprehension quiz at the end. Tap any word to hear its pronunciation.
🎙️
AI Chat — Talk to Lián
Lián is your AI conversation partner. Chat in Chinese, ask grammar questions in English, or request a scenario like "practice ordering at a restaurant." Lián detects your level, corrects mistakes gently, and adjusts complexity automatically. The Grammar module has a Practice with Lián button on every pattern.
✍️
Writing Practice — Stroke Order
Learn to write each character the correct way. Watch animated stroke-order diagrams, then switch to Memory mode — the character disappears and you draw it yourself on the canvas. Hit Reveal to compare. Stroke count and radical info are shown for every character.
词
Vocabulary Bank — Browse & Search
Browse 7,500+ words by HSK level or any of 99 themed categories. Search by Chinese, pinyin, or English. Tap any word to open the detail panel with a listen button and the Hanzi Decomposition panel — hover each component to see its radical, meaning, and etymology.
🧩
Grammar — 18 HSK Patterns
18 essential grammar patterns (HSK 2–5), each with a plain-English explanation, a colour-coded Syntax Formula, an interactive word-slice (hover a word to highlight its role), a drag-and-drop word-order puzzle, a fill-in-the-blank quiz, and a one-click button to practice the exact pattern with Lián live.
🎯
Quizzes — Test Your Knowledge
Randomised multiple-choice quizzes drawn from the entire vocab bank or a specific HSK level. Pick the correct meaning, hear the right answer, earn XP. Streak bonuses multiply your score when you get several correct in a row — keep your combo going!
🔊
Tone Guide — Master the 4 Tones
Mandarin has four tones — 马 (horse), 妈 (mother), 骂 (scold) differ only in tone. The Tone Guide shows pitch contour charts and plays audio examples for each tone. The Audio flashcard mode trains tone recognition by playing the word before revealing the character.
📈
Progress — XP, Streaks & Achievements
Everything earns XP: flashcards (+5), quizzes (+10), stories (+15), writing (+8). Level up from 初学者 (Beginner) to 汉字大师 (Master). Your daily goal bar, streak counter, and skill radar update live. Unlock achievement badges and track your vocabulary mastery by HSK level.
Study Tips
学
💡 Tones matter! The same syllable with different tones means completely different words. Start learning tones from day one.
🔤 Pinyin first, characters second. Use pinyin as a stepping stone — but always aim to recognise the characters too.
🧠 Spaced repetition is the single most effective study method. Mark missed flashcards and review them more often.
🗣️ Speak every day. Click the 🔊 buttons throughout the app and mimic what you hear — your ear and mouth need practice together.
🏆 Streak = progress. Open the Progress tab daily to keep your streak alive and unlock achievements.

Numbers & Money
数字与金钱 · shùzì yǔ jīnqián — Click any number to hear it
0 – 10
11 – 99
Hundreds, Thousands & Beyond
💰 Money & Prices 钱
⚡ Quick Drill
?
What does this number mean?
✅ 0 correct ❌ 0 wrong 🎯 Accuracy: —
How Chinese numbers work:
Numbers are beautifully logical! 11 = 十一 (shí yī = ten + one). 25 = 二十五 (èr shí wǔ = two tens + five).
100 = 百 (bǎi). 1,000 = 千 (qiān). 10,000 = 万 (wàn) — Note: Chinese uses 万 not "ten thousand" as a unit!
Money: The unit is 元 (yuán), colloquially called 块 (kuài). 1 yuan = 10 角 (jiǎo) = 100 分 (fēn).
二 vs 两 (the most common mistake):
 • 二 (èr) = the numeral "2" — used in counting, phone numbers, math: 一、二、三… 第二 (second) 二月 (February)
 • 两 (liǎng) = "two of something" — used before measure words: 两个人, 两本书, 两杯茶
 • Rule of thumb: before a measure word → always 两. Standalone counting → 二.
Phone numbers & codes: Read digit by digit. 138 0012 3456 → yāo sān bā líng líng yī èr sān sì wǔ liù. Note: 1 is said as 幺 (yāo) in phone numbers to avoid confusion with 7 (qī).
Prices: ¥25.50 → 二十五块五 (or 二十五元五角). Fractions drop the last zero: ¥3.00 → 三块钱.

Stroke Order Rules

Top → Bottom
Write strokes from top to bottom.

Left → Right
When strokes are side by side, go left to right.

Horizontal before vertical
Horizontal strokes cross before vertical ones bisect.

Outside before inside
Draw the enclosing frame before the inside.

Center before sides
In symmetrical characters, center comes first.

Close the box last
Bottom of an enclosure is the final stroke.

🧠 Memory Mode

How it works
You see the pinyin and meaning — but no character outline. Draw it from memory on the blank canvas.

Reveal & Self-grade
After drawing, tap Reveal to see the correct character overlaid on your attempt, then rate yourself.

Pro tip
Work through Watch → Trace a few times first, then challenge yourself in Memory mode!

Lessons & Grammar
语法与实用课程 · Interactive lessons on time, numbers, money, and Chinese grammar
1

First Tone ˉ

ā
High and level. Chao: 55. Like a sustained singing note.
猫 书 飞
māo · shū · fēi
2

Second Tone ´

á
Rising. Chao: 35. Like asking "Really?" in English.
人 来 学
rén · lái · xué
3

Third Tone ˇ

ǎ
Low dipping. Chao: 214. Dips down then rises. In isolation only — sandhi common.
水 好 我
shuǐ · hǎo · wǒ
4

Fourth Tone `

à
Sharp falling. Chao: 51. Forceful — like a command or surprise.
是 去 爱
shì · qù · ài
0

Neutral Tone

a
Short, unstressed, no fixed pitch. Follows context. Used for particles and word endings.
吗 呢 了
ma · ne · le
📐 Tone Change Rules (Sandhi)
3rd + 3rd → 2nd + 3rd
Two consecutive 3rd tones: the first becomes 2nd tone.
你好 written as nǐhǎo, pronounced níhǎo  ·  可以 kěyǐ → kéyǐ  ·  所以 suǒyǐ → suóyǐ
不 bù changes
Before a 4th-tone syllable: bù → bú
不是 bú shì  ·  不去 bú qù  ·  不对 bú duì
Before tones 1, 2, 3: stays bù    不来 bù lái  ·  不好 bù hǎo
一 yī changes
Before 1st, 2nd, 3rd tone: yī → yì    一天 yì tiān  ·  一年 yì nián  ·  一点 yì diǎn
Before 4th tone: yī → yí    一个 yí gè  ·  一次 yí cì
In isolation or as ordinal: stays yī
3rd tone before non-3rd
In natural speech, isolated 3rd tone is often shortened to a low tone (Chao: 21) without the final rise.
This is why 马 mǎ in 马上 sounds "flat low" rather than dipping-then-rising.
🔤 Minimal Pairs — Same syllable, 4 tones
Click any character to hear it. Notice how different they sound!
mā/má/mǎ/mà
bāo/báo/bǎo/bào
tāng/táng/tǎng/tàng
shī/shí/shǐ/shì
guō/guó/guǒ/guò
📝 Pinyin System — Initials & Finals
Pinyin uses 21 initials (consonants) and 36 finals (vowels/endings). Every syllable = initial + final + tone.
Initials (声母 shēngmǔ)
bp mf dt nl gk h jq x zhch shr zc s yw
Key Finals (韵母 yùnmǔ)
a / ia / ua — open "ah"
e / ie / üe — mid/open
i / ui / ing — "ee" sound
o / uo / ou — "oh" / "ow"
u / iu / un — "oo" sound
ü / üe / ün — French "u"
an / ian / uan — nasal -an
ang / iang / uang — open nasal
en / in / un — closed nasal
eng / ing / ong — velar nasal
ai / uai — "eye"
ao / iao — "ow!"
ü note: Written as u after j, q, x, y (always pronounced ü). Written as ü after l, n only.
i note: After zh/ch/sh/r/z/c/s, the i is a "buzzing" sound — not the bright "ee".
🔗 Common Tone Combinations with Examples
1+2 中国 Zhōngguó (China)
3+3→2+3 你好 nǐhǎo (hello)
4+0 谢谢 xièxie (thank you)
4+4 汉字 hànzì (Chinese character)
2+0 朋友 péngyou (friend)
2+2 学习 xuéxí (to study)
3+1 老师 lǎoshī (teacher)
1→2+4 一个 yígè (one — sandhi)
Tap any pair to hear it spoken.
⚠️ Common Tone Confusions
买 mǎi (3rd, buy) vs 卖 mài (4th, sell) — opposite tones, opposite meanings!
问 wèn (4th, ask) vs 吻 wěn (3rd, kiss) — confusing these in public is memorable.
鸡 jī (1st, chicken) vs 几 jǐ (3rd, how many) — both sound like "jee" but differ in pitch.
贵 guì (4th, expensive) vs 鬼 guǐ (3rd, ghost) — calling the price a ghost is a common slip.
🔢 Chao Tone Numbers
Linguist Yuen Ren Chao described tones on a 1–5 scale: 1 = lowest, 5 = highest. This gives a compact numeric notation for pitch contours.
55
1st tone — high level
stays at top
35
2nd tone — rising
mid to high
214
3rd tone — dipping
down then up
51
4th tone — falling
high to low
🎧 Tone Practice Sentences — tap to listen
Each sentence is colour-coded by tone. Try to hear the tones before clicking!
妈麻马骂吗
māo má mǎ mà ma — all 5 tones, same syllable family
我爱中国
Wǒ ài Zhōngguó — 3rd 4th 1st 2nd · I love China
你好吗？
Nǐ hǎo ma? — 3rd→2nd(sandhi) 3rd neutral · How are you?
我想吃北京烤鸭
Wǒ xiǎng chī Běijīng kǎoyā — 3 3 1 3 1 3 1 · I want to eat Peking duck
他不是老师
Tā bú shì lǎoshī — 1 2(sandhi) 4 3 1 · He is not a teacher

Quick starters
连 Lián
Your AI Mandarin companion
About Lián
连 is a Mandarin-speaking AI companion. She'll always reply in Chinese first, then English — perfect for immersive practice.

Tips:
• Type in English and she'll respond in Chinese
• Type in Chinese for full immersion
• Ask her to correct your mistakes
• Request vocabulary or grammar help
• Click 拼 to toggle pinyin on her replies
